data.title: What Does the Market Think About Nu Holdings Ltd?
data.description:
Nu Holdings Ltd's (NYSE:NU) short interest as a percent of float has risen 3.94% since its last report. According to exchange reported data, there are now 108.32 million shares sold short, which is 2.9% of all regular shares that are available for trading. Based on its trading volume, it would take traders 3.41 days to cover their short positions on average.

Why Short Interest Matters

Short interest is the number of shares that have been sold short but have not yet been covered or closed out. Short selling is when a trader sells shares of a company they do not own, with the hope that the price will fall. Traders make money from short selling if the price of the stock falls and they lose if it rises.

Short interest is important to track because it can act as an indicator of market sentiment towards a particular stock. An increase in short interest can signal that investors have become more bearish, while a decrease in short interest can signal they have become more bullish.

Nu Holdings Ltd Short Interest Graph (3 Months)

As you can see from the chart above the percentage of shares that are sold short for Nu Holdings Ltd has grown since its last report. This does not mean that the stock is going to fall in the near-term but traders should be aware that more shares are being shorted.

Comparing Nu Holdings Ltd's Short Interest Against Its Peers

Peer comparison is a popular technique amongst analysts and investors for gauging how well a company is performing. A company's peer is another company that has similar characteristics to it, such as industry, size, age, and financial structure. You can find a company's peer group by reading its 10-K, proxy filing, or by doing your own similarity analysis.

According to Benzinga Pro, Nu Holdings Ltd's peer group average for short interest as a percentage of float is 0.68%, which means the company has more short interest than most of its peers.

This article was generated by Benzinga's automated content engine and was reviewed by an editor.

data.title: Nano Nuclear Seeks Commercial Allies For Space Nuclear Power
data.description:
Nano Nuclear Energy Inc. (NASDAQ:NNE) stock rose Thursday as the company outlined a new space power initiative.

The firm is seeking external expertise to advance its space fission power technology.

Nano Nuclear has issued a formal Request for Information to identify partners for future surface fission power system development.

The request, titled "Nano Nuclear Energy NASA Surface Fission Power Partnership Capabilities," aims to gauge industry experience in tech development, system integration, and operational support related to Nano Nuclear's LOKI MMR microreactor.

Context Of The Initiative

The United States is expanding its space exploration ambitions beyond low Earth orbit toward the Moon and ultimately Mars.

Reliable power sources are critical for long-duration missions, permanent infrastructure, and scalable exploration architectures.

These efforts align with a 2025 executive order calling for sustained lunar presence by 2028 and nascent outpost components by 2030.

NASA and U.S. partners are emphasizing technologies that can operate independent of intermittent energy sources, reduce operational risk, and support continuous surface activities.

Fission-based systems are seen as the most scalable solution to these goals.

This RFI helps position Nano Nuclear to support national goals beyond Earth's orbit, said Jay Yu, founder and chairman. He said responses will help the company plan the next phases of reactor integration and maturation.

Technical Strategy

The LOKI MMR is being developed by Nano Nuclear Space and is aimed at meeting space power challenges like extended lunar night or Martian winter, autonomous operations, and passive safety.

The system targets 300 kilowatts to 1 megawatt of electrical output and is scalable down to 100 kilowatts for space applications.

The reactor uses helium and supercritical CO₂ coolants and TRISO fuel enriched with LEU or HALEU.

It is engineered to weigh less than 15 metric tons and operate for up to 10 years without refueling, providing continuous power for demanding extraterrestrial environments.

Strategic Outlook

Nano Nuclear said responses to the RFI will shape its outreach to commercial, government, and international partners.

The company believes the feedback will guide planning for surface fission power development aligned with NASA and the Department of Energy exploration objectives.

NNE Price Action: Nano Nuclear Energy shares were up 2.08% at $35.08 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image by Piotr Swat via Shutterstock

data.title: Self Storage Could Be Limited, Analyst Recommends This Buying Stock
data.description:
There are limited demand catalysts, such as housing turnover, in 2026, and this year is likely to be similar to 2025, "with portfolio footprint and market performance having an outsized impact on earnings," according to Goldman Sachs.

The Public Storage Analyst: Analyst Caitlin Burrows maintained a Buy rating on Public Storage (NYSE:PSA), while cutting the price target from $332 to $321.

Same-store revenue growth in 2026 could range from -1.0% to +1.2%, which excludes the "potential outsized performance" of Los Angeles, which would impact Public Storage more due to geography mix, "if wildfire pricing restrictions are removed," Burrows said in the note.

Check out other analyst stock ratings.

Public Storage is outperforming rival Extra Space Storage Inc (NYSE:EXR) in the same markets, she added.

"Despite being in various overlapping markets, performance can vary greatly," which highlights the importance of location as well as company strategy and execution, the analyst stated.

Burrows said that both Public Storage and Extra Space Storage have stronger results than the broader market. "While PSA has more explicitly called out the headwind wildfire restrictions have on the LA market and pricing strategy, EXR faced larger headwinds in LA in 3Q25," she further wrote.

Price Action: PSA shares had risen by 0.80% to $ 290.07 at the time of publication on Thursday.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Dave Ramsey Dismantles 'Lies And Bad Advice' About Mortgage Rates, Inflation, Car Loans And More. 'That's Straight-Up Illegal'
data.description:
Dave Ramsey is tired of the financial fearmongering. In a fiery speech, the personal finance personality went after what he calls the nonstop “lies and bad advice” making people feel helpless and broke in today's economy. And he’s not pulling any punches.

“The lies and the bad advice are everywhere out there,” Ramsey said. “You’re death scrolling into oblivion and it’s just not true.”

Ramsey Says The Numbers Tell A Different Story

He addressed claims that inflation and mortgage rates are historically bad, calling it misinformation. “Inflation in 2024 was 3.4%. Inflation in 22 was 6.2%,” he said. “In 1982, it was 7.4%. In 1980, it was 12.4% and had been double digits for almost the previous 10 consecutive years.” On mortgage rates, he added, “Interest rates in 1982 were 17.66% for a house. Today, we're bouncing down there next to the bottom of five. Not hardly 17.”

Don't Miss:

  • Fast Company Calls It a ‘Groundbreaking Step for the Creator Economy' — Investors Can Still Get In at $0.85/Share
  • Sam Altman Says AI Will Transform the Economy — This Platform Lets Investors Back Private Tech Early

As for the idea that the average American can’t make ends meet anymore? Ramsey wasn't buying it. “Median household income is now $83,000. And the average household expenses are 78,” he said. “The math says otherwise.”

He also pushed back on the claim that buying a car without a loan is impossible. Ramsey cited new data showing the average new car costs $42,000, with average monthly payments at $748.

“We have an affordability crisis on our housing because we have a car payment in the driveway. And you got screwed by Citibank and Ford Motor Company.”

Budgeting Isn’t Just For Broke People

“Only poor people do a budget? That’s a lie,” he said. Ramsey Solutions surveyed over 10,000 millionaires and found 93% of them did a monthly budget, according to Ramsey. “So there’s your little statistical analysis. Cause and effect. Correlation. Causation,” he said.

Trending: Americans With a Financial Plan Can 4X Their Wealth — Get Your Personalized Plan from a CFP Pro

Ramsey also ripped into trendy tax hacks. “Bad advice: set up an LLC and run all of your personal expenses through it. You can write them off,” he said. “No, you can’t. That’s straight-up illegal. Your house electricity is not a business expense. Ever heard of an audit? They’ll come take your stuff from you.”

On Credit Cards, Retirement And The Myth Of ‘Passive’ Income

He called out the logic of fearing a 5% mortgage while carrying a credit card with a 22.8% interest rate. “It’s a bit illogical, people.”

And to those saying retirement is out of reach, Ramsey broke it down. “If you invest from age 22 to age 67, just $70 a month, you’d have a million dollars at prevailing market rates,” he said.

He also mocked the idea that starting a business is a quick path to easy money. “Easy and passive? Bull crap,” he said. “Only on TikTok.”

From Losing Millions To Preaching Common Sense

Ramsey shared how he once lost everything after building a $4 million portfolio with too much debt. He called his collapse a wake-up call. “I had built a house of cards and I shouldn’t be shocked that it fell,” he said. “I gave them access to my life and let them screw me.”

His turnaround came from applying biblical money principles and sticking to simple habits. “Live on less than you make. Get out of debt. Have a plan. Be generous,” he said. “Grandma’s common sense.”

Take Control And Block Out The Noise

Ramsey closed by telling the audience they have more opportunity today than ever before. “You have so stinking much freedom if you’ll turn off the stupid talking heads and the white noise and go be somebody,” he said. “It’s unbelievable what you can do.”

For those looking to stop guessing and start planning, services like Domain Money offer professional guidance from certified financial planners. That extra layer of support could be the difference between staying stuck and finally taking control.

“Stop listening to the Hope Steelers,” Ramsey said. “They’re wrong. The data says they’re wrong.”

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: BlackRock Posts Q4 Beat; Analyst Projects High-Single-Digit Revenue Growth
data.description:
BlackRock Inc (NYSE:BLK) reported its fourth-quarter results ahead of expectations, with 12% growth in organic base fees, according to Goldman Sachs.

The BlackRock Analyst: Analyst Alexander Blostein reiterated a Buy rating and price target of $1,234.

The BlackRock Thesis: The company reported net revenues of $6.33 billion, beating Street expectations by 3%, and adjusted earnings at $13.16 per share, topping consensus of $12.09 per share, Blostein said in the note.

Check out other analyst stock ratings.

BlackRock generated net flows of $342 billion, representing around 9% long-term organic growth, he added.

"Encouragingly, the mix of flows skewed towards higher fee rate products, driving 12% organic base fee growth during the quarter (and bringing full year organic base fee growth to 9%) – at the higher end of investors’ expectations," the analyst wrote.

The company's organic base fee growth is averaging more than 7%, with growth in Tech revenues "likely supporting a high-single digit revenue growth algo and a mid-teens EPS growth algo over the next few years," she further stated.

BLK Price Action: Shares of BlackRock had risen by 4.64% to $1,142.51 at the time of publication on Thursday.

Image: Shutterstock
